tossups.question: The composer of this set of orchestral works swindled the publishers Artaria and Forster by selling exclusive rights to both companies. It is not known why the third of these works was nicknamed for the three-word Latin phrase that their composer habitually wrote on the dedication page of his scores. The first of these works derives its nickname from the bagpipe-like drone on C in its finale. These are the most performed works premiered by a European conductor of African descent, who led a Masonic orchestra called the Olympic Lodge; that conductor is Chevalier de Saint-Georges. The second of these symphonies derives its nickname from a dotted-rhythm oboe ostinato that sounds like clucking. For 10 points, “The Bear” and “The Hen” are among what group of six symphonies by Joseph Haydn, written the decade before his “London” Symphonies?
tossups.question_sanitized: The composer of this set of orchestral works swindled the publishers Artaria and Forster by selling exclusive rights to both companies. It is not known why the third of these works was nicknamed for the three-word Latin phrase that their composer habitually wrote on the dedication page of his scores. The first of these works derives its nickname from the bagpipe-like drone on C in its finale. These are the most performed works premiered by a European conductor of African descent, who led a Masonic orchestra called the Olympic Lodge; that conductor is Chevalier de Saint-Georges. The second of these symphonies derives its nickname from a dotted-rhythm oboe ostinato that sounds like clucking. For 10 points, "The Bear" and "The Hen" are among what group of six symphonies by Joseph Haydn, written the decade before his "London" Symphonies?
tossups.answer: Joseph Haydn’s “Paris” Symphonies [or Joseph Haydn’s Symphonies Nos. 82–87; prompt on Joseph Haydn’s symphonies by asking “Which set?”]
tossups.answer_sanitized: Joseph Haydn's "Paris" Symphonies [or Joseph Haydn's Symphonies Nos. 82-87; prompt on Joseph Haydn's symphonies by asking "Which set?"]

tossups.question: A G minor piece of this type includes a dotted oboe passage in its second movement representing an animal’s jerky head movements, and is part of a set of six premiered by conductor Chevalier de Saint-Georges (“san-ZHORZH”). The final movement of one piece of this type features a five-part fugato centered around the notes [read slowly] C, D, F, E. An F sharp minor piece of this type ends with musicians blowing out their candles and leaving the stage. Joseph Haydn (“HIGH-dun”) included a sudden G major chord in the second movement of his 94th piece of this type, allegedly to wake up any sleeping audience members. For 10 points, name these orchestral pieces that include Jupiter by Mozart and Farewell and Surprise by Haydn.
tossups.question_sanitized: A G minor piece of this type includes a dotted oboe passage in its second movement representing an animal's jerky head movements, and is part of a set of six premiered by conductor Chevalier de Saint-Georges ("san-ZHORZH"). The final movement of one piece of this type features a five-part fugato centered around the notes [read slowly] C, D, F, E. An F sharp minor piece of this type ends with musicians blowing out their candles and leaving the stage. Joseph Haydn ("HIGH-dun") included a sudden G major chord in the second movement of his 94th piece of this type, allegedly to wake up any sleeping audience members. For 10 points, name these orchestral pieces that include Jupiter by Mozart and Farewell and Surprise by Haydn.
tossups.answer_sanitized: symphony [or symphonies; accept Jupiter Symphony; accept Farewell Symphony; accept Surprise Symphony]

packet.name: F - McGill E + Edinburgh C + Imperial A + Murray State A + CWRU A + Georgia Tech A + Virginia Tech A + NYU B

tossups.question: Some of the earliest pieces in this genre were written by the Milanese composer Antonio Brioschi [bree-OS-key], who wrote 26 of them. Seventy-eight of these pieces were written by Giovanni Battista Sammartini, who helped shift this genre away from an earlier “Italian” style. With an ensemble partly named the Olympic Lodge, Chevalier de Saint-Georges [che-val-YAY de sahnt george] premiered a set of six of these pieces in Paris. These pieces originally were (*) three movements and were used as overtures in opera houses prior to the developments of Mannheim School composer Johann Stamitz. Classical-era composers began writing pieces in this genre with an opening sonata, a slow movement, a minuet or scherzo, and an allegro or rondo finale. For 10 points, name this long genre for full orchestra, such as Haydn's “Farewell.”
tossups.question_sanitized: Some of the earliest pieces in this genre were written by the Milanese composer Antonio Brioschi [bree-OS-key], who wrote 26 of them. Seventy-eight of these pieces were written by Giovanni Battista Sammartini, who helped shift this genre away from an earlier "Italian" style. With an ensemble partly named the Olympic Lodge, Chevalier de Saint-Georges [che-val-YAY de sahnt george] premiered a set of six of these pieces in Paris. These pieces originally were (*) three movements and were used as overtures in opera houses prior to the developments of Mannheim School composer Johann Stamitz. Classical-era composers began writing pieces in this genre with an opening sonata, a slow movement, a minuet or scherzo, and an allegro or rondo finale. For 10 points, name this long genre for full orchestra, such as Haydn's "Farewell."

packet.name: F (Stanford B, Virginia Tech, Yale B)

tossups.question: Composer and genre required. One of these pieces by this composer opens with the stormy ascending half notes G, B-flat, C-sharp, and D, followed by dotted figures falling to low D then a dramatic silence. In the Vivace finale of one of these pieces, a drone in the low strings accompanied by gracenotes on the downbeat evokes a Dudelsack used to accompany street performances, giving the piece its nickname. A set of six of these pieces was conducted at their premiere at the Olympic Lodge by Joseph Bologne, Chevalier de Saint-Georges, and subsequently gained nicknames like “The Hen” and “The Bear.” A set of these pieces named for London includes one in which a startling fortissimo chord interrupts its quiet Andante second movement. For 10 points, “Farewell” and “Surprise” are examples of what orchestral pieces written by an Austrian composer?
tossups.answer: symphonies by Franz Joseph Haydn [prompt on symphonies or Paris symphonies or London symphonies by asking “by what composer?”]
tossups.answer_sanitized: symphonies by Franz Joseph Haydn [prompt on symphonies or Paris symphonies or London symphonies by asking "by what composer?"]

bonuses.leadin: Out of jealousy, Mozart allegedly based the villain Monostatos in The Magic Flute on this composer. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Out of jealousy, Mozart allegedly based the villain Monostatos in The Magic Flute on this composer. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this French composer of eight symphony-concertantes and the opera L'Amant anonyme (“la-MONT a-noh-NEEM”). This Guadeloupe-born composer’s status as a free man of colour did not stop him from conducting Le Concert Olympique (“luh con-SEHR oh-lam-PEEK”).

Under the baton of Saint-Georges (“san-ZHORZH”), Le Concert Olympique premiered a set of six symphonies by Haydn named for this French city. Saint-Georges was himself the director of an opera company based in this city before he was removed due to racist objections.

The fourth of Haydn’s Paris symphonies is named due to it being a favourite of this student of Christoph Gluck, who was also close friends with Saint-Georges, and the female dedicatee of Luigi Cherubini’s (“ke-roo-BEE-nee”) Requiem in C Minor.
parts_sanitized: Name this French composer of eight symphony-concertantes and the opera L'Amant anonyme ("la-MONT a-noh-NEEM"). This Guadeloupe-born composer's status as a free man of colour did not stop him from conducting Le Concert Olympique ("luh con-SEHR oh-lam-PEEK").

Under the baton of Saint-Georges ("san-ZHORZH"), Le Concert Olympique premiered a set of six symphonies by Haydn named for this French city. Saint-Georges was himself the director of an opera company based in this city before he was removed due to racist objections.

The fourth of Haydn's Paris symphonies is named due to it being a favourite of this student of Christoph Gluck, who was also close friends with Saint-Georges, and the female dedicatee of Luigi Cherubini's ("ke-roo-BEE-nee") Requiem in C Minor.
answers: Joseph Bologne, Chevalier de Saint-Georges [accept either underlined part]
Marie Antoinette [or Marie Antoinette Josèphe Jeanne] (The symphony is subtitled “La Reine”, or “The Queen”.)
answers_sanitized: Joseph Bologne, Chevalier de Saint-Georges [accept either underlined part]
Paris [accept Paris symphonies; accept Opera de Paris]
Marie Antoinette [or Marie Antoinette Josephe Jeanne] (The symphony is subtitled "La Reine", or "The Queen".)

bonuses.leadin: This composer, the illegitimate son of a slave and a colonial French planter, was sent away from Guadeloupe as a child to study in France. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this 18th-century French composer and violinist. A 2020 New York Times article criticized the common practice of referring to this man as the “Black Mozart.”

A dubious theory holds that Boulogne was the inspiration for the villainous Monostatos in this Mozart opera. Other theorists claim that Empress Maria Theresa is the basis for this opera’s “Queen of the Night.”
As conductor of the Concert Olympique, Saint-Georges commissioned this group of six orchestral pieces. This set includes pieces nicknamed “The Queen,” “The Bear,” and “The Hen.”
answers_sanitized: Joseph Boulogne [or the Chevalier de Saint-Georges]
Paris Symphonies [prompt on symphonies by Haydn]

bonuses.leadin: Chevalier de Saint-Georges, often considered the first successful classical composer of color, composed eight of these pieces as one of the first and foremost promoters of the genre. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this genre featuring two or more soloists, exemplified by an E-flat major Mozart piece for violin, viola and orchestra. It was replaced by the double and triple concertos.

After an affair in which his skin color kept him from directing the Paris Opera, Saint-Georges likely played in private with this pianist and royal. Haydn's fourth Paris symphony is nicknamed “the Queen” in honor of her.

The related Baroque concerto grosso was likely introduced by Arcangelo Corelli, whose eighth and best-known concerto grosso is nicknamed for this occasion. A large vocal work whose first part is nicknamed for this occasion features an instrumental Sinfony and Pifa alongside lyrics by Charles Jennens.
answers: sinfonia concertante [or sinfonie concertante; or symphonie concertante; prompt on partial]
Marie Antoinette [or Maria Antonia Josepha Johanna; prompt on partial]
Christmastime [or Christmas Day; or Christmas Eve] (The oratorio is Handel's Messiah)

bonuses.leadin: This man succeeded Arthur Saint-Léon as Maître de Ballet at the Imperial Theatres in Saint Petersburg. For ten points each:
parts: Name this French ballet master and choreographer who collaborated with Ludwig Minkus on the ballets Don Quixote and La Bayadère.
Petipa choreographed the premiere of this Tchaikovsky ballet, based on a fairy tale by Charles Perrault and features the characters Carabosse and king Florestan.

Petipa worked with librettist Jules-Henri Vernoy de Saint-Georges and composer Cesare Pugni on this ballet, in which an English lord and his servant experience an opium dream while sheltering from a sandstorm in a pyramid. Petipa’s role as Ta-Hor in the first performance of this ballet was his last as a dancer.
answers_sanitized: Marius (Ivanovich) Petipa [accept Victor Marius Alphonse Petipa]
The Pharaoh's Daughter [accept La fille du pharaon and Doch' faraona]
